article.title: CMS clarifies Stark Law value-based arrangement exceptions following stakeholder feedback
article.summary: CMS has issued FAQ guidance clarifying that physician compensation tied to value-based payment arrangements remains permissible under the Stark Law's value-based exception (42 CFR 411.357(aa)) when the physician group acts as a value-based enterprise (VBE) and meets the documentation requirements.
article.rawContent: CMS's updated FAQ addresses common practice questions on the Stark Law value-based exceptions: (1) compensation can be partially based on quality metrics, total cost of care, or population health goals if the arrangement is documented as part of a value-based enterprise; (2) the writing requirement is satisfied by a contract or written policy that identifies the VBE participants and the value-based purpose; (3) the in-office ancillary services exception is unchanged for routine practice-internal referrals; (4) groups with VBE arrangements must maintain documentation for 6 years to satisfy potential audit. CMS notes that practices uncertain about applicability should consult counsel before tying any physician compensation to designated health services referrals.
